title: Who's Most Likely To Be Traded Away From Maple Leafs At 2026 NHL Trade Deadline?
description: While McMann and Laughton are at the top of the list, there still seems to be the possibility that both get re-signed.
fullText:
The Toronto Maple Leafs are in a spot they haven't been in quite some time.

With the playoffs looking unlikely at this point, the Maple Leafs could be sellers at the NHL trade deadline for the first time since 2016, the last time they missed the playoffs.

They've got several unrestricted free agents, as well as a few other players who could bring value back to Toronto, whose prospect and draft cupboard is pretty bare. This is a chance to re-tool so that next season, they're primed to get back into the playoff picture.

But who's most likely to go? Who's destined to be on another team in their quest for destiny later this spring?

Of the Maple Leafs' UFAs this summer, Bobby McMann's name seems to come up most often.

The 29-year-old is having a career season, approaching highs in goals, assists, and points. His speed, strength, and shot are also attributes that teams could value in the playoffs.

McMann burst onto the scene in the NHL with Toronto in 2023-24. His 15 goals through 56 games had him in sixth among Maple Leafs players that year. In every season since, McMann's game has grown more prominent, eventually landing him in an almost regular top-six role with Toronto this season.

But with how the Maple Leafs' year has gone, they could get considerable value for McMann if they were to move him.

There have been reports that the Maple Leafs would like a first-round pick for the forward, and it would be incredible if Toronto could recuperate something like that. At the same time, though, could there be value in keeping McMann?

Report: Maple Leafs Looking For First-Round Pick For Bobby McMann The Maple Leafs don't have their first-rounder this year or next year, after trading them away.

He's in his prime. Toronto also doesn't have very many players behind McMann who play a similar game. It seems to be very much still in play.

At the end of it all, it depends on what McMann wants money-wise and what the Maple Leafs are willing to pay.

Nevertheless, the forward is at the top of our list for a reason: NHL teams will definitely value his services. And if you can get a first-round pick for him, who could say no to that?

At $3.5 million for two more seasons after this one, Oliver Ekman-Larsson is an attractive defenseman for teams that are pushing for the Stanley Cup.

The 34-year-old's eight goals and 35 points in 60 games are the most among Maple Leafs defensemen and the fifth-most on the team. Ekman-Larsson can also quarterback the power play, which he's done plenty this season with Toronto. He's also coming off an Olympics with Team Sweden.

Don't forget the defenseman also won it all with the Florida Panthers in 2025.

While there'll likely be plenty of suitors inquiring about what it'll take to acquire Ekman-Larsson, the Maple Leafs may still want to keep him. He has a 16-team no-trade list, too, so the defenseman does have a bit of say in what occurs with his future.

Ekman-Larsson is arguably Toronto's best defenseman. They don't have to move him if they don't want to. But because of the lack of assets, maybe GM Treliving looks at trading him before the deadline.

One year ago, Scott Laughton was ready for the inevitable of being traded away from the Philadelphia Flyers, the only NHL team he's ever known.

Now, he's facing the same reality again.

Like McMann, the veteran forward is approaching July 1 without a contract. Since being acquired by the Maple Leafs last spring for Nikita Grebenkin and a 2027 first-round pick, Laughton has tallied 10 goals and 16 points through 62 games.

"I didn't think I'd probably be in this situation," Laughton said on Saturday, ahead of their game against the Ottawa Senators. "It’s tough to think about, but at the same time, you got to come in and do a job to the best of your ability. I want to be in the fight with these guys."

‘It’s Tough To Think About’: Scott Laughton Addresses Trade Speculation As Maple Leafs Near NHL Trade Deadline After being acquired to help a playoff push just one year ago, Oakville native Scott Laughton now faces the reality of being traded away from his hometown team as the Maple Leafs shift from buyers to sellers.

What teams might value with Laughton is his leadership and faceoff abilities. His 56.7 percent faceoff win percentage is among the top 20 in the NHL. Toronto could also retain some of his $3 million salary, which could entice teams even further.

He and McMann are also candidates to be re-signed by the Maple Leafs. But what does general manager Brad Treliving value more? Getting assets, or bringing those two back on new contracts?

Toronto has three picks in this year's draft, none of which are in the first two rounds. At some point, you've got to recoup some assets. Those two guys could give you the best chance at doing so.

There was chatter about trading Brandon Carlo last summer, and it appears it's picked up again ahead of this deadline.

The positives: he's a right-shot defenseman, big, strong, and only makes $3.485 million with the Maple Leafs. Carlo is still young, too, at 29.

It's clear things haven't worked out as much as everyone hoped when the Maple Leafs acquired Carlo for Fraser Minten and a conditional 2026 first-round pick last spring. But it's certain teams will still value the 6-foot-5 defenseman.

Maybe Toronto retains even more salary to heighten the price tag?

With how young Carlo still is, I'd bet the Maple Leafs keep him and hope for a better season next year. If that occurs, they're in a pretty good position with a big, strong, and mobile defender who can also play big minutes on the penalty kill.

Among Toronto's forward group, Calle Jarnkrok is the only other UFA aside from Laughton and McMann.

Here's the thing: if the Maple Leafs aren't going to make the playoffs, which it seems like they won't, every UFA should be made available. Jarnkrok has six goals and one assist in 38 games this season, which isn't the most flattering when you look at his $2.1 million annual average value.

However, if Toronto tells teams they'd be open to retaining some of that salary, maybe a club goes for the Swede?

Jarnkrok is a strong penalty killer and can provide goals from time to time. On the other side, though, is: will he be an effective player for teams headed to the playoffs?

He hasn't had much success with Toronto in recent years.

Either way, Toronto needs to get anything they can for the forward. If a team offers a late pick, Treliving has got to take it.

title: The Jeremiyah Love to New Orleans Dream May Be Over, So What Now?
description: The Saints have a major need at RB, but the options are becoming extremely limited
fullText:
New Orleans Saints HC Kellen Moore values a dominant run game in his offense, but this was far from the case last season. A combination of poor offensive line play in the run game and disappointing performances/injuries among the RBs kept the Saints from finding a consistent rushing attack. Their best bet was to rely on the QBs to attack opposing defenses on the ground, which worked at times with Spencer Rattler and Tyler Shough, both good runners, but they are not good enough rushers to rely on it.

To take as much pressure as possible off of Moore and Shough, the running game needs to be improved this offseason. Although fixing the offensive line first is extremely important, another talented RB needs to be added to the roster. This could be from free agency or the draft, but now, there is a major issue. The options available are becoming increasingly limited, and the Saints may have to settle for a lower-tier option.

In free agency, RB Breece Hall is set to be tagged or extended by the New York Jets. Hall was one of the best options for New Orleans, and now, Kenneth Walker and Travis Etienne are the only remaining "high-end" options. Etienne has been strongly linked to the Chiefs, and Walker may be too expensive for where the Saints' roster stands.

The next top-end option is the 2026 NFL Draft, and that would be Jeremiyah Love. Before combine week, there was an understanding that Love would be available at 8...well, not anymore. At the NFL Combine, Love had an exceptional performance. Even before his workouts, there were rumors spreading that teams with top-5 selections had interest in the star RB. Then, the workouts made it more likely for Love to be selected in that range.

In the 40-yard dash, Love ran a 4.36. Just to really explain how insane this was, star Detroit Lions RB Jahmyr Gibbs, at 5-foot-9, 199 lbs, ran the same time. Love is 6-feet tall and 212 lbs. Adding this type of performance with the meeting he had with teams, there is now an extremely low chance that Love will be available at pick #8.

For the Saints, they may have wanted to fix RB before the NFL Draft anyway, but not even having that option could really hurt New Orleans. Now, the Saints' outlook for the free agency RB market is much worse, and paying for Walker could be by far the best move for Moore.

title: Venus Williams brought some Hall of Fame tennis greatness to Austin's ATX Open | Golden
description: Tennis legend Venus Williams is 45, the age when most former elite athletes have moved on. Yet she's still grinding, still playing and left her mark in Austin.
fullText:
Venus Williams could be the most overlooked legend in the history of American sports.

Among the pantheon of great athletes, hers is a unique story because she once owned a sport yet ended up being surpassed by possibly the greatest tennis player ever: her own sister.

Williams played to an adoring crowd at Austin's Westwood Country Club in two matches at the ATX Open this past week and left players and fans alike in astonishment at her ability to still bang with some of the best young players in the game. Even at 45 years old, Williams' willingness to meet the physical demands of professional tennis is truly inspiring.

MORE CEDDY: Why Muschamp’s hire makes sense for Texas football

It’s part of what makes the Williams family so iconic in American lore. Two working-class parents in a crime-ravaged section of South Central Los Angeles believed tennis was a portal to help their two youngest daughters find a better life away from the potentially devastating effects of the crack epidemic that overran America's poorest neighborhoods. Those public courts on the corner of Atlantic Avenue and Compton Boulevard unknowingly served as the birthplace of the greatest run in American tennis history.

This family’s contributions to American sports and its history is unparalleled. How many inner-city parents can claim they raised and coached two daughters to play what’s been largely a country club sport and guided both to the top of the profession?

Venus and Serena both reached No. 1 in the world, both won Olympic gold medals and both transcended the sport they represented with footprints in social media, fashion, entrepreneurship, entertainment and charitable causes.

Richard Williams and his then-wife Oracene defied the odds and created one of the most improbable rags-to-riches tale in the history of tennis and beyond. Venus and Serena took over the sport, starting with Venus’ professional debut at age 14 in 1994 and stretching across another three decades. The sisters earned millions through wins and endorsements while big sister Venus followed in the legendary Billie Jean King’s footsteps in demanding equality at the bank.

MORE CEDDY: Florida coach respects Texas but will Horns make NCAAs?

Venus’ op-ed column one day before the 2006 Wimbledon tournament in the UK’s The Times lobbied for the All-England Club to award equal prize money for men and women after the other slams — the Australian, French and US Opens — had already done so.  It carried substantial weight because it came from a three-time champion who was among the most recognizable athletes on the planet at the time.

One year later, Williams captured a fourth Wimbledon singles title in 2007 and her check — around $1.4 million—  was identical to that of men’s champion Roger Federer.

“It’s still the best moment of my career,” Williams told reporters last summer after losing to Elina Svitolina in the first round.

MORE TENNIS: Best photos of Venus Williams at ATX Open

One year later, she hopes to receive a wild card to tennis' biggest tournament. When I spoke to her Tuesday, she still wore the same competitive face of someone 20 years younger. My goal was to get her to open up about the tremendous legacy she carries with her family and the impact it's had generations of players and fans alike.

Do you ever take inventory on what you, Serena and your family have accomplished and given to American sport and Black culture at large?

The answer came from someone fresh off a loss who still wants to win tennis wars against teenagers, 20somethings and the biggest foe of them all, Father Time.

“Just being in the heat of the battle, it’s hard to do that,” Williams said. "But it’s definitely been an honor to be able to contribute back to the game and to the U.S. and just in general.”

These days, she's no longer a huge voice in the sport, just an older player happy to be playing in the background.

"I exited that a while ago," she said. "I feel like I contributed a lot over the years and now I just play Venus."

Her opponent on Tuesday, 32-year-old Ajla Tomljanovic  — who beat Serena in her final match on the tour (the third round of the 2022 US Open) —  was a teenager when the sisters stood atop the sport. She gushed when asked about their impact.

“They were trailblazers who did everything first,” Tomljanovic said. “They demand a change, from their tennis to their fashion. Girls started wearing jewelry to the court once they did it. It was almost like whatever they did, they made it OK so everyone else could do it.”

In Tuesday’s second set of her first-round loss, Williams was facing a break point and hit a scorching winner down the line and the crowd at Westwood Country Club’s Center Court roared its approval.

Tomljanovic nodded in admiration.

“Come on,” Venus muttered in a barely audible tone.

Williams is 45, an age where most former all-world athletes have moved on to other pursuits. But her desire to compete at a high level still burns deep. She's no longer a threat to win a major, but she puts in the work as if she was still ranked No. 1, in which she was for 21 weeks in 2002, the sixth-longest run at the top for an American-born player.

“I don't think I will be at that level at 45,” Tomljanovic said. “I told my coach I think I’ll be in a wheelchair if I play that well, so I’m not even going to attempt that. Though it’s inspiring, I’m closer to finishing at 35 than 45.”

Williams then took her leave at the ATX Open after losing in the first round of doubles with former Texas Longhorns star Peyton Stearns, who's enjoying her best ATX Open yet. But Williams' presence resonated with those who spent time with her in Austin last week or watched her show glimpses of the form that once made her the best in the sport.

After the loss, the duo flashed a Hook’em Horns sign together.

“I taught her that,” Stearns said.

The sport should enjoy Venus 2.0 while it can because we’re getting an extra dose of a a walking, talking Hall of Famer. The owner of seven major singles titles — five Wimbledons and two US Opens — and another 14 doubles titles and three Olympic gold medals to boot, Williams has nothing to prove. She’s beaten the best the game has to offer and is winning her biggest fight against Sjögren's Syndrome, a long-term autoimmune disease that was diagnosed in 2011.

“She's not doing it to make money,” said ATX Open founder Bryan Sheffield. “She loves the game. That's what's so beautiful. You can see in her face and the way she shakes hands, even though she just lost a match. She might play for five more years.”

It’s been 29 years since ATX Open tournament director Christo Van Rensburg played his final Wimbledon. He won two singles titles, but made his bones in doubles where he won 20 titles — nine with longtime partner Paul Annacone — and reached a career high ranking of No. 5.

The South African was at his final Wimbledon in 1997 and had already lost in the men’s doubles in the first round while 17-year-old American prodigy Venus Williams was playing her first Wimbledon and looking for a mixed doubles partner after her first-round loss in singles. The doubles wild card entry lost on Court 18 to Mahesh Bhupathi and Rika Hiraki in three sets.

While Van Rensburg walked off the Wimbledon court for the last time, a star was about to be born. Three years later, Williams became the first Black woman since Althea Gibson in 1958 to hoist Wimbledon's Venus Rosewater Dish after taking out defending champion Lindsay Davenport.

SOFTBALL: How Teagan Kavan returned to form

Decades later. Williams and Van Rensburg reconnected at last week's ATX's pre-tournament players party at the Driskill Hotel and talked about old times as they sat with Williams’ newlywed husband Andrea Preti.

“When they reached out to me and said Venus wanted to play at the ATX Open, I thought how cool that would be,” said Van Rensburg, an Austin resident. “When she arrived here, I waited at the entrance for her. It was good to see her after all of these years and she’s still out there competing hard.”

No conversation with Venus would be complete without asking about her younger sister, who is, for my money, the greatest to ever do it.

Serena Williams has been in the headlines for a startling transformation through a weight loss drug and is eligible to return to the tour after completing the drug-testing protocol — though she has been coy about coming back at age 44.

Days before her mother’s disappearance made national headlines, "TODAY" host Savannah Guthrie asked Serena on the air if she was coming back.

“I mean, really?” Serena replied. “Are you asking this on the 'TODAY' show? Oh my gosh.”

Then she laughed. When pressed for a yes/no answer, Serena said she's having fun and enjoying life.

But she didn’t say she was staying retired.

TEXAS BASEBALL: Jim Schlossnagle reveals UT's scouting strategy

When I posed the question to Venus and asked about the possibility of a Williams doubles team reunion at a grand slam, Big Sis didn’t sugarcoat the answer.

“Wouldn't that be cool?” she said. “I never see her practicing, though. I think to come back, you have to get out there, so we'll see if she can put some work in. I mean, of course playing for exercise is nice, but there's a lot of hours that goes into it, so we’ll see what happens.”

Spoken like a true champion still dedicated to her craft.

If anyone can make a comeback and put a scare into these young’uns, it’s Serena.

The sport could use a Sister Act 2.

title: Suns lookahead. Phoenix eyes season sweep over Sacramento Kings
description: The Phoenix Suns face the Sacramento Kings for the final time in the 2025-26 regular season March 3 at Golden 1 Center in Sacramento.
fullText:
The Phoenix Suns look to sweep their regular season series against the Sacramento Kings as they face them for the final time in the regular season March 3 at Golden 1 Center in Sacramento.

The Suns opened the 2025-26 regular season Oct. 22 with a 120-116 comeback win over the Kings at home. Phoenix trailed by as many as 20 points before rallying to victory.

The Suns won the second matchup, 112-100, Nov. 26 in Sacramento. Mark Williams and Collin Gillespie each scored 21 points as Phoenix led by as many as 25 points and never trailed in the game.

The Suns took the third meeting, 129-102, Jan. 2 after a four-game road trip. Devin Booker scored 33 points. Phoenix scored 23 points off 21 Sacramento turnovers.

The Suns will follow their final game against the Kings with a three-game homestand starting March 5 against the Chicago Bulls. This will be the first Suns-Bulls matchup of the season. They face each other again April 5 in Chicago.

Phoenix then takes on New Orleans on March 6 in the second of a back-to-back. The Suns are 3-0 against the Pelicans, winning the first matchup, 121-98, on Nov. 10.

Grayson Allen scored a career-high 42 points in just 27 minutes. He also hit a career-high 10 3s on 15 attempts to set a Suns record for most made 3s in a single game.

The Suns then took two from the Pelicans in New Orleans in a back-to-back Dec. 26-27. Booker scored 50 total points in the two games. He played high school basketball in Moss Point, Mississippi, which is a little more than 90 minutes from New Orleans.

Phoenix concludes the homestand March 8 against the Charlotte Hornets. They have yet to play each other this season. The second matchup is set for April 2 in Charlotte.

Have opinions about the current state of the Suns? Reach Suns Insider Duane Rankin at dmrankin@gannett.com or contact him at 480-810-5518. Follow him on X, formerly Twitter, at @DuaneRankin.

Support local journalism: com today.

This article originally appeared on Arizona Republic: Phoenix Suns look to complete sweep over Sacramento Kings

title: What was star Boston Celtics forward Jaylen Brown like as a college player?
description: Thankfully for those of us looking, the folks behind the "No Ceilings NBA" YouTube channel put together a clip of some of his NCAA highlights.
fullText:
What was star Boston Celtics forward Jaylen Brown like as a college player? The Georgia native surprised many by electing to go to Cal for his choice of team at the NCAA level, where the spacing did not favor Brown's style of play at that point in his career. But the future Boston champion still managed to stand out enough to end up a top-three pick in the 2016 NBA Draft.

But what WAS his game like at Cal, you ask? Thankfully for those of us looking to scratch this very specific basketball itch, the folks behind the "No Ceilings NBA" YouTube channel put together a clip of some of his NCAA highlights at Cal.

Take a look at the clip embedded below to see how Brown played while at the college level for yourself.

title: Our Diamondbacks silver anniversary celebration series debut | Opinion
description: Our nine-month special series details all that went into the Arizona Diamondbacks capturing their first and only World Series championship.
fullText:
That’s normally the time it takes to decide a Major League Baseball game. But not always. There’s no clock in baseball, after all, so sometimes things will have to play out until the score is settled and the victorious team is finally determined.

MLB crowned a World Series champion 25 years ago, the 2001 Arizona Diamondbacks, and as we look back to commemorate the silver anniversary of their achievement, it’s important to reflect on how the journey unfolded — from start to finish.

That’s why we are unveiling a unique nine-month special series, starting today (Sunday, March 1).

We'll bring fresh perspectives and updated memories from key figures who helped shape, guide and lead the 2001 Diamondbacks. We will roll out a different installment at the beginning of every month from now through November.

Fittingly, we’re calling this series: “The 2001 Diamondbacks: Nine innings to a World Series title.”

Part 1: How Bob Brenly's simple rules led to Diamondbacks' World Series title

Our first inning explores the construction of the 2001 team with insight from then-chairman Jerry Colangelo, general manager Joe Garagiola Jr. and others for a potential run at the title in mind. The series continues with how early-season success helped shape what was to come, and later, explores the first-year managerial magic of Bob Brenly and why things all seemed to come together.

We also will recapture the moments of greatness and grandeur from stars such as Randy Johnson, Luis Gonzalez and Curt Schilling, explore the push toward the postseason and focus on some of the unheralded names and faces who played a larger role than many expected.

Oh, and of course, we will recount the playoff run and unforgettable World Series win over the vaunted New York Yankees, along with memories of the Sept. 11 terrorist attacks that changed the world.

There is a ton to unpack here, obviously, so we hope everyone reading will sit back, embrace the conversation, and enjoy these next nine months — whether you were here when it happened or want to know the details if you weren’t.

I was fortunate enough to be there for the better part of it, and there are several moments I’ll never forget — from how the whole franchise got off the ground years earlier, when the idea was just a hopeful thought brought to Colangelo by me in 1993, and then others the very next day.

Bank One Ballpark (now Chase Field) was the place to be during that championship season, and just as great as the games themselves were the daily clubhouse interviews with everyone from Gonzo, the Big Unit, Mark Grace, Tony Womack, Craig Counsell, Jay Bell, Matt Williams, Steve Finley and the rest.

What began with big-money investments by Colangelo and Garagiola Jr. to build the 2001 roster ended with one of the most dramatic Game 7 victories of all time on Nov. 4, followed by a crowd of 300,000 or more celebrating Arizona’s victory parade in downtown Phoenix three days later.

And that was only part of the overall story.

Some folks might not remember that the Commissioner’s Trophy was badly damaged during the party, and although it was later repaired to its original glory, Diamondbacks fans will always recall how Johnson and a healthy Schilling for a full season became the most powerful 1-2 pitching punch in baseball that season. They owned the National League, finishing 1-2 in virtually every major pitching statistic.

A pair of 18-win months in May and June helped solidify Arizona’s grand expectations. Winning eight of their final 11 games, the Diamondbacks captured their second National League West Division title, going 92-70 to finish two wins ahead of the second-place Giants.

They took out the Cardinals and Braves in the NL divisional and championship series, respectively.

Johnson collected his third consecutive Cy Young Award. Schilling was second in the balloting. The Arizona aces shared World Series Most Valuable Player honors.

Gonzalez, meanwhile, had a career year with a stunning 57 home runs and 142 RBIs. He began his onslaught by slugging 13 homers in April, tying Ken Griffey Jr. for the most in major league history to start a season. But it’s how he ended the season in a memorable Game 7 of the World Series that will never be forgotten.

It was the bottom of the ninth inning. Gonzalez strode to the plate with one out, the bases loaded and the Yankees’ infield drawn in.

“Someone has to tell me we just beat the New York Yankees and Mariano Rivera,” Grace said amid the wild, on-field celebration, “because I still don’t believe it.”

Believe it. It’s been 25 years, but for the next nine months, you can relive the moments and the memories like it was only yesterday by following our special series — “The 2001 Diamondbacks: Nine Innings to a World Series Title.”

Reach McManaman at bob.mcmanaman@arizonarepublic. Follow him on X, formerly Twitter: @azbobbymac and listen to him live every Tuesday at 3:30 p.m. on Roc and Manuch with Jimmy B on ESPN 620 (KTAR-AM) and every Thursday on the Doug Franz Unplugged podcast via Apple or Spotify.

azcentral.com/newsletters/manage/ for complete coverage.

title: Arizona State men's basketball in final stretch of regular season
description: Arizona State athletics schedule from March 2-7, including men's basketball at home.
fullText:
Bobby Hurley and the Arizona State men's basketball team are nearing the end of the season with two regular-season games left.

And it's going to be a tough slate before the March 10-14 Big 12 tourney at the T-Mobile Center in Kansas City.

The Sun Devils will play their final home game on March 3 against Kansas. The Jayhawks have one of the better defenses in the Big 12, holding opponents to an average of 38.7% on field goals entering their Feb. 23 matchup with Houston.

Kansas was also among the conference's top three rebounding teams.

The ASU-Kansas game will be shown on FS1, with tip-off scheduled for 7 p.m. MST.

ASU will then close the season at Iowa State on March 7, another team ranked among the best in the country. The Cyclones were holding opponents to 65.1 points per game.

The game will tip off at noon and will be shown on FS1.

title: NFL Writer reveals 2025 Kansas City Chiefs rookie class report cards
description: NFL Writer reveals 2025 Kansas City Chiefs rookie class report cards | @EdEastonJr
fullText:
The Kansas City Chiefs' rookie class included some standout players who weren't initially drafted by the team. NFL.com Editor, Gennaro Filice, shared his grades of the Chiefs' draft class, including thoughts on two specific rookies.

Filice graded the Chiefs with a B-, listing the splits for games played and started in 2025. Undrafted free agent left tackle Esa Pole was also included in his grading system. Kansas City ranks 20th among 32 NFL teams on the list.

"A Day 1 starter on Patrick Mahomes' blind side, (Josh) Simmons looked the part, yielding just one sack in the first five games of the season, per PFF. But then he missed four gameswhile tending to a family matter," wrote Filice. "After returning to the starting lineup in mid-November, Simmons continued to thrive ... until a wrist injury ended his season on Thanksgiving. I remain high on Simmons' raw talent, but he's not gaining fans in the "availability is the best ability" crowd. (Nohl) Williams, on the other hand, was active for all 17 games, though his defensive snap totals varied during the first three months of the season."

Simmons had an impressive rookie season when he was available to play and contribute in the starting lineup. The rookie class had extra time on the field to end the 2025 season, given the team's 6-11 finish and its playoff ineligibility.

This article originally appeared on Chiefs Wire: NFL Writer reveals 2025 Kansas City Chiefs rookie class report cards

title: Why is Wesley Fofana not playing for Chelsea vs. Arsenal?
description: Chelsea will face off against Arsenal in a crucial match on Sunday, March 1 without key defender Wesley Fofana.
fullText:
Chelsea will face Arsenal in a crucial match on Sunday, March 1 without key defender Wesley Fofana.

The French center back will be serving a one-game suspension after he was sent off in last weekend's 1-1 draw against Burnley.

Already carrying a yellow card, Fofana lunged in for a dangerous tackle on Burnley midfielder James Ward-Prowse in the 71st minute at Stamford Bridge. The 25-year-old was shown a second yellow card, reducing Chelsea to 10 men.

That red card would prove costly, as Burnley grabbed a late equalizer through Zian Flemming to earn an unlikely 1-1 draw.

In the aftermath of his sending-off, Fofana revealed that he had been racially abused on social media.

“It was obviously a tough day for Wes, firstly what happened in the game and then the fall-out, the racist abuse he got online. Wes is a tough guy, a good guy," Chelsea head coach Liam Rosenior said at his pre-game press conference.

Last weekend's draw left Chelsea in fifth place as it travels to north London for a match against league-leading Arsenal.

Fofana has made 26 total appearances this season under Rosenior and Enzo Maresca.

With Fofana out, Trevoh Chalobah and Tosin Adarabioyo will be the favorites to start at the Emirates Stadium.

Jorrel Hato, Mamadou Sarr, Josh Acheampong and Benoît Badiashile are also options for Rosenior.

This article originally appeared on USA TODAY: Why is Wesley Fofana not playing for Chelsea vs. Arsenal?

title: Karl Mecklenburg reacts to Bo Nix getting a new play caller
description: Broncos QB Bo Nix will have a new play caller in 2026. This was Karl Mecklenburg's reaction.
fullText:
Karl Mecklenburg originally shared this post on his social media pages, and it was re-shared here with permission.

What’s Bo Nix’s shelf life with a play caller? He left Auburn after 3 years and transferred to Oregon for 2 years. He got drafted by the Broncos and played for 2 years with Sean Payton as the play caller. Next season Davis Webb will be the Broncos offensive play caller. Maybe it’s just typical football player and coach turnover, but maybe it’s Bo. I do know that when the quarterback/play caller connection is tight it makes a huge difference in team success. Patrick Mahomes and Andy Reid are a great example.

I was the Broncos defensive captain with Joe Collier and then Wade Phillips calling the defensive plays. In both cases, over time, I developed an understanding of what they wanted in specific situations. With both defensive coordinators there were offensive looks where I’d adjust the defense based on that knowledge. With Wade, there would be times he was still looking at his play sheet as the other team broke the huddle. I knew what he was going to call, so I’d just call it with no sideline confirmation. That unspoken mental connection takes more than 2 years.

From all reports Davis Webb is the next young gun in offensive play calling. I hope he and Bo have the chance to work together for many years and develop that telepathy that only time together allows. If so, it will take Nix’s play to the next level. Go Broncos!

Mecklenburg played for the Denver Broncos from 1983-1994, earning six Pro Bowl nods and five All-Pro selections during his decorated career in the NFL. Mecklenburg is now a motivational keynote speaker. You can book Mecklenburg on his website.

Social: Follow Broncos Wire on Facebook and Twitter/X! Did you know: These 25 celebrities are Broncos fans.

This article originally appeared on Broncos Wire: NFL: Karl Mecklenburg reacts to Bo Nix getting a new play caller

title: Will you try to go to the Detroit Lions’ game in Germany?
description: The Detroit Lions are playing in Munich, German in 2026? How do fans feel about it?
fullText:
It’s been a long week of Detroit Lions news. Down at the NFL Combine, general manager Brad Holmes, head coach Dan Campbell, offensive coordinator Drew Petzing, and defensive coordinator Kelvin Sheppard all spoke with the media. In the middle of that, Taylor Decker announced he’s coming back for an 11th NFL season. Then there’s the developing situation with Terrion Arnold.

It’s easy to forget that the Lions also announced this week that they’re playing in Munich, Germany in 2026.

For some, that news comes as a celebration. It’s an opportunity for the Lions to continue to grow their brand globally, and an opportunity to see the Lions live in an exciting part of the world. For others, it’s an inconvenience and (likely) a loss of a game that would otherwise be played in Ford Field.

Today’s Question of the Day is:

My answer: Yes, that is absolutely the plan.

For one, I think it should be a great chance to produce some good content for the site, providing insight into the process of what it’s like for a team to prepare and pull off an international game.

But more selfishly, I want to travel. I’ve only been to Europe once in my life, and I’m eager for the opportunity to go again. Germany seems like an excellent place to start, but I hope my international travel doesn’t end there. If the Lions get a bye week after their Germany game—like most teams get after an international game—I plan on extending my trip in Europe to some surrounding countries and cities.

Either way, it’s a chance to experience another culture and experience the world in a new way.

What are your thoughts on the Lions’ Munich game? Plan on making the trip? Share your thoughts in the comment section below.

title: Back on 'home' turf: Varun Chakravarthy chance to silence his critics
description: Varun Chakravarthy returns to Eden Gardens, a venue where he has excelled, as a key weapon for India's T20 squad. Despite recent indifferent form, both Indian and West Indies management acknowledge his wicket-taking ability. The team also expressed strong support for Rinku Singh following his father's passing.
fullText:
KOLKATA: The one thing that strikes you about Varun Chakravarthy is his complete lack of facial expressions. Just by looking at him, you wouldn’t know if he has taken a wicket or gone for a six. Rather, his teammates seem to be more excited about his achievements than him. But that takes nothing away from him being an integral part of India’s T20 squad.

Varun is now back at the venue where he has thrived in the past. The 34-yearold leg-break googly bowler, an important part of the Kolkata Knight Riders team in the IPL, will consider Eden Gardens his home. Of his 100 IPL wickets, 99 are for KKR, with this iconic venue contributing quite a few of those. This is not lost on the Indian team management and assistant coach Ryan ten Doeschate agrees Varun could be one of their potent weapons here.

“Varun obviously had a lot of success here,” ten Doeschate said on Saturday. “I think it’s pretty standard how we’ve utilized Varun so far. He bowls one over in the powerplay. He’s our attacking option as well.”

However, is Varun still as effective as he was in his early days? In the last two games against South Africa and Zimbabwe, he conceded 82 runs for his two wickets. Are the batters approaching him in a different way? Ten Doeschate is inclined to agree.

“We’re also aware that teams are now starting to play him a little bit differently. So we have some other strategies to deploy him in different facets of the game. But generally speaking he’s someone we turn to for wickets and he’s been really good at that and expect more of the same on Sunday.”

The West Indies won’t take Varun lightly despite his relatively indifferent form. “Last time I checked I think he was the No. 1 bowler in T20 cricket,” West Indies coach Darren Sammy said. “We respect every opposition. We have our plans against each bowlers and vice versa, our bowlers versus their batsman. We got to play a complete game.”

Back in the familiar surroundings of the Eden Gardens, the occasion could just be the perfect setting for Varun to silence doubters. And an early wicket in his powerplay over could well give India the edge.

Team firmly behind Rinku

Ten Doeschate said that the team is standing firmly with batter Rinku Singh, who lost his father to cancer on Friday. “It’s obviously a very sad time and he’ll continue to get the love and support from everyone,” he said.

title: Former Vols by the numbers on Day 3 of 2026 NFL combine
description: A look at how former Vols performed on Day 3 of the 2026 NFL combine.
fullText:
The third day of the NFL scouting combine concluded at Lucas Oil Stadium in Indianapolis, Indiana. A total of 319 prospects were invited to Indianapolis, including seven former Vols.

Former Tennessee quarterback Joey Aguilar, wide receiver Chris Brazzell II, defensive lineman Bryson Eason, defensive back Colton Hood, defensive lineman Joshua Josephs, tight end Miles Kitselman and defensive back Jermod McCoy were invited to the 2026 NFL scouting combine.

Place-kickers, defensive linemen and linebackers participated in drills on Day 1. Tight ends and defensive backs participated in on-field drills on Day 2, while quarterbacks, wide receivers and running backs took part in drills on Day 3. Offensive linemen will conclude drills on Sunday.

Below are results from former Vols on Day 3.

Before he transferred to Tennessee in 2025, Aguilar played at Diablo Valley Community College (2021-2) and Appalachian State (2023-24). In 13 games at Tennessee, he completed 272-of-404 passing attempts for 3,565 yards, 24 touchdowns and 10 interceptions, while rushing for 101 yards and four touchdowns. Aguilar did not participate in drills at the NFL scouting combine.

Before he transferred to Tennessee in 2024, Brazzell II played at Tulane from 2022-23. In 25 games for the Vols, he recorded 91 receptions for 1,350 yards and 11 touchdowns.

Follow Vols Wire on Facebook and X (formerly Twitter).

This article originally appeared on Vols Wire: How former Vols performed on Day 3 of 2026 NFL combine

title: Sam Ericsson, Josie Biteler voted Argus Leader Athletes of the Week
description: Lincoln's Sam Ericsson and Washington's Josie Biteler were voted by readers as the Argus Leader's Athletes of the Week from Feb. 16 to 21.
fullText:
Sioux Falls Lincoln boys basketball player Sam Ericsson and Sioux Falls Washington girls basketball player Josie Biteler were voted by readers as the Argus Leader's Athletes of the Week from Feb. 16 to Feb. 21.

Ericsson earned the boys Athlete of the Week with 70% of the vote.

Ericsson got hot from deep in Lincoln's statement win over Roosevelt on Tuesday, Feb. 17. The senior sharpshooter made five 3-pointers and finished the night with 15 points.

Biteler earned the girls Athlete of the Week with 48% of the vote.

Biteler scored 14 points in the Warriors' win over Sioux Falls Roosevelt on Friday, Feb. 20. The sophomore guard scored seven points in the third quarter, when Washington started opening the game up.

This article originally appeared on Sioux Falls Argus Leader: Sam Ericsson, Josie Biteler voted Argus Leader Athletes of the Week

title: Fog forces cancelation of men's World Cup super-G in Germany. Race will be rescheduled in France
description: GARMISCH-PARTENKIRCHEN, Germany (AP) — Persistent fog Sunday forced the cancelation of the first men’s World Cup super-G since the Milan Cortina Olympics.  Organizers lowered the start gate and delayed the race several times but ultimately called it off "due to the present weather situation with limited visibility from the fog,” the International Ski & Snowboard Federation said.  FIS men's race director Markus Waldner told Austrian TV that the race will be rescheduled for March 13 in Courchevel, France, which hosts the last weekend of speed races before the World Cup Finals in Norway the following week.
fullText:
GARMISCH-PARTENKIRCHEN, Germany (AP) — Persistent fog Sunday forced the cancelation of the first men’s World Cup super-G since the Milan Cortina Olympics.

Low clouds in the upper part of the Kandahar course reduced visibility and made a safe race impossible.

Organizers lowered the start gate and delayed the race several times but ultimately called it off "due to the present weather situation with limited visibility from the fog,” the International Ski & Snowboard Federation said.

FIS men's race director Markus Waldner told Austrian TV that the race will be rescheduled for March 13 in Courchevel, France, which hosts the last weekend of speed races before the World Cup Finals in Norway the following week.

Marco Odermatt holds a commanding 158-point lead in the super-G season standings with three races left. The Swiss standout has 425 points, followed by Vincent Kriechmayr of Austria on 267.

The men’s World Cup continues with a slalom and a GS in Kranjska Gora, Slovenia, next weekend.

AP skiing: https://apnews.com/hub/alpine-skiing

title: Sixers vs. Celtics game preview: How to watch, lineups, broadcast info
description: The Philadelphia 76ers will take on the Boston Celtics on Sunday night for the first time since Nov. 11.
fullText:
The Philadelphia 76ers will head up to TD Garden to take on the Boston Celtics on Sunday, and if you're wondering how you can watch all the action live, you've come to the right place. The Sixers and Celtics have played three times thus far--Philadelphia has won two of those matchups--but they have not played since Nov. 11 in Xfinity Mobile Arena.

The Celtics are a team that has been one of the best in the league despite not having Jayson Tatum all season long as he recovers from an Achilles tendon injury. They enter this contest as the No. 2 seed in the East and the Sixers will need to be ready for the challenge of what's to come. It will be on Tyrese Maxey to lead the way for a group who will be looking to build on their current 3-game winning streak.

Here's when and where you should tune in to see the matchup:

Channel: NBC Sports Philadelphia, Peacock

Sixers: OUT: Paul George (league suspension), Johni Broome (right knee meniscus tear), Joel Embiid (right oblique strain)

Celtics: OUT: Jayson Tatum (right Achilles repair) QUESTIONABLE: Baylor Scheierman (left thumb fracture)

Sixers: Classic Edition (Black)

Celtics: City Edition (White with Gold trim)

title: Steelers free agent primer: Running back
description: The Steelers are going to hit free agency with their best offensive player as a free agent, and this means running back is a high priority.
fullText:
Free agency is upon us and the Pittsburgh Steelers are going into it with nearly $45 million in salary cap space. General Manager Omar Khan spoke at the NFL Scouting Combine and definitely gave the impression the Steelers would be willing to spend some money this time around. In our free agency primer, we take a look at the Steelers position-by-position.

The Steelers currently have Jaylen Warren, Kaleb Johnson, Lew Nichols and Max Hurleman on the roster and under contract for the upcoming season. Warren signed a new contract last offseason and is set to be the lead back again this season. Meanwhile, Kaleb Johnson came in as a rookie last season and was a complete disappointment. We should know fairly quickly if he's in the team's long-range plans.

The Steelers have to make a decision on Kenneth Gainwell but he might not give them a chance. After being the team's best player on offense and team MVP last season, Gainwell is going to cash in with a team where he can be the feature back and with Warren already in place, he could be gone.

All the Steelers fans want a guy like Breece Hall or Kenneth Walker but these players don't want to share reps with Warren or possibly Johnson as well. The best mid-tier guys who Pittsburgh might look at are players like JK Dobbins, Rico Dowdle or Jerome Ford to give the Steelers depth but a player who won't rock the boat if they aren't a full-time starter.

We don't see much here in terms of players the Steelers would want to forfeit draft capital for. The Saints have Kendre Miller coming off a torn ACL, but he was showing steady improvement until that point last season. He's a guy who could be on the radar as a late addition to be the team's No. 3 back.

We expect the Steelers to address running back in the draft and hope they can hit where they seemingly missed in 2025. The guy we like the most outside of Notre Dame's Jeremiyah Love is Arkansas' Mike Washington Jr. He's got a frightening blend of size and speed and is a very good receiver out of the backfield. The other guy is Nebraska's Emmett Johnson, who reminds us an awful lot of Warren and might pair up perfectly with him.

This article originally appeared on Steelers Wire: Steelers free agent primer: Running back

title: Green wins LPGA title with husband as caddie
description: Australia's Hannah Green wins the LPGA's Women's World Championship in Singapore after her husband steps in as her caddie.
fullText:
HSBC Women's World Championship - final round leaderboard

-14 H Green (Aus); -13 A Kim (US); -11 P Bouchard (Fra), A Yin (US), M Lee (Aus); -10 H Ryu (Kor); -8 L Duncan (US); -7 A Iwai (Jpn), R Takeda (Jpn)

Selected others: -6 L Woad (Eng), C Hull (Eng), M Rhodes (Eng)

Australia's Hannah Green won the LPGA's Women's World Championship in Singapore after her husband stepped in as her caddie.

With Green's usual caddie unable to leave the USA while he applies for a green card, her husband Jarryd Felton - who is a professional golfer - filled the role for the second time this year.

Green, 29, carded a three-under-par 69 on Sunday to finish one shot clear of American Auston Kim and win this title for a second time.

She married Felton in 2024 and has also caddied for her husband in the past.

"It was absolutely a team effort over the last two weeks, and it's very special to share my seventh win, which is also my favourite number, with him," said Green.

"My usual caddie is applying for a green card and couldn't leave the US, so it wasn't part of the plan for the season. Luckily, my husband was able to pick up the bag for me."

Green started the day tied for the lead with three-time major champion Minjee Lee, but took control with an early birdie followed by an eagle on the eighth hole.

Green sank three more birdies on the final nine, and - despite a nervous finish after bogeying her final two holes - ended on 14 under overall to win this tournament for the second time in three years, having triumphed in 2024.

"I was feeling nervous and told Jarryd a couple of times that I wasn't comfortable. He reminded me to take a deep breath, have a snack or even sip some water," she said.

"I haven't played with this kind of adrenaline in a long time, and we handled it really well."

England's Charley Hull, Mimi Rhodes and Lottie Woad all finished in a tie for 10th on six under.

title: Borussia's set-piece streak continues against Bayern – but so does Kane's
description: Signs of general stability: Borussia Dortmund lost two competitive matches in a row for the first time under Niko Kovac. BVB suffered their first home defeat of the season, the last Bundesliga team to...
fullText:
Signs of general stability: Borussia Dortmund lost two competitive matches in a row for the first time under Niko Kovac. BVB suffered their first home defeat of the season, the last Bundesliga team to do so. Their previous home defeat was almost a year ago.

Back again: Nico Schlotterbeck had not played in the last four competitive matches for BVB (suspension, injury). He was back in the starting line-up for the top-of-the-table clash, scoring his fourth goal of the season and equalling his personal record for a single season.

On target: Daniel Svensson scored his second goal of the season. He previously scored on Matchday 5 in Mainz.

Close: Harry Kane also scored his 24th Bundesliga penalty. Gregor Kobel was only the third keeper to at least get a hand to the ball... Kane scored twice in his fourth Bundesliga game in a row, equalling the historic record set by Lothar Emmerich (1967 for Dortmund) and Tomislav Maric (2001 for Wolfsburg).

title: 'We're gonna be better,': Texas A&M HC confronts tough 11–1 setback
description: Texas A&M HC Michael Earley called the 11–1 rout a harsh early-season lesson, emphasizing better execution and a stronger effort in the series finale.
fullText:
No. 1 UCLA delivered a blunt reminder of the sport’s hierarchy on Saturday night, outclassing No. 23 Texas A&M in an 11–1 run‑rule defeat in Game 2 of the Amegy Bank College Baseball Series.

The Aggies weren’t just beaten — they were overwhelmed. UCLA’s lineup forced long at‑bats, fouled off tough pitches, and refused to give away a single plate appearance. Every mistake found a gap, every missed location was punished, and every inning felt like a grind for A&M’s pitching staff. On the other side, the Aggies pressed at the plate, squaring balls up only to watch them find a Bruin defender.

A&M was out‑hit 14–2, struck out seven times, and rarely threatened with runners in scoring position. The margin could have been even worse. UCLA stranded seven runners compared to A&M’s three. The lone bright spots came on the mound, where Gavin Lyons allowed just one run in three innings of relief, and true freshman Cole Hubert — despite a rough frame — flashed enough to project long‑term upside.

For Michael Earley and his team, the message is clear. A beatdown like this stings, but it also exposes exactly where the gaps are. If the Aggies want to position themselves for a postseason push, they’ll need to absorb this lesson quickly, tighten up on the mound, and respond with urgency.

"Yeah, I never wanna get my ass kicked, but at the same time, it's like, sometimes you can take something good from it, right? But in the end, you gotta play better baseball. It's never acceptable to lose like that. We're gonna be better, we're gonna bounce back tomorrow, and we're gonna give a good effort tomorrow."

Even with Wesley Jordan and Chris Hacopian sidelined, this loss falls more on the pitching staff’s inability to limit contact. UCLA didn’t overpower A&M — they simply executed at a higher level, inning after inning.

Contact/Follow us @AggiesWire on X (formerly Twitter) and like our page on Facebook to follow ongoing coverage of Texas A&M news, notes and opinions. Follow Jarrett Johnson on X: @whosnextsports1.

This article originally appeared on Aggies Wire: Texas A&M HC Michael Earley confronts tough 11–1 setback against UCLA

title: How to watch Fulham vs Tottenham Hotspur live: Stream link, TV channel, latest team news, prediction
description: It's take two for Tottenham Hotspur boss Igor Tudor when he leads relegation-threatened Spurs into Craven Cottage for a Sunday scrap in the Premier League.
fullText:
It's take two for Tottenham Hotspur boss Igor Tudor when he leads relegation-threatened Spurs into Craven Cottage for a Sunday scrap in the Premier League.

Spurs have spiraled down the Premier League table with just two wins since the start of November and three-straight losses as part of an active nine-match winless run. Sixth on the table after nine games, Tottenham Hotspur are now just four points clear of the bottom three and what would signify their first ever relegation from the Premier League.

Fulham, meanwhile, have been solid this season apart from two month-long swoons. They're hoping the second one was ended and not paused by last week's win at Sunderland.

The Cottagers have a forgiving fixture list and could still make a run into Europe — their 37 points have them 10th, three off seventh place and eight back of fifth.
